architecture_fit.note: Minimal single softmax head; saturation_score=Σp² is mechanistically the softmax-Jacobian trace (gradient flow), directly characterizing the saturation regime the goal names.

baseline_comparison.note: Exemplary failing strawman: the no-exp relu-linear head is algebraically scale-invariant, stays flat across the sweep, and its AUROC collapses to chance — isolating exp as the mechanism.

faithfulness.note: Real GPU autograd measurement of |∂‖attn·v‖²/∂logits| shows the gradient collapsing exactly where C→1, plus the exp ablation; honestly notes synthetic limits and proposes the real-model patch analogue.

operating_range.note: Sweep spans 0.1→100 (3 orders of magnitude); concentration rises monotonically, AUROC=1.0, entropy correlation ≈1.0 across the full range.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-built analytic mechanism (Σp² and the relu-linear ablation) — no training at all, the circuit is written out by hand.

visual_judgement.note: Three coupled, legible panels: detection contrast with both AUROCs in-legend, log-log gradient collapse, and a scale-slider attention heatmap confirming near-one-hot rows.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies each panel against the goal — concentration (=saturation score) on the y-axis, no-exp baseline, gradient as the mechanistic distinguisher from mere confidence.

notes: An exceptionally well-rounded analytic attempt: it reframes saturation as softmax-Jacobian (Σp² = 1 − tr J) collapse, matches the oracle (AUROC=1.0, entropy correlation ≈1.0), and backs the claim with both a real GPU autograd gradient-collapse measurement and a clean algebraic no-exp ablation that fails to chance. The visualisation is legible and each panel is justified against the goal, and the README is honest about the synthetic task's faithfulness limits. Every rubric item is satisfied at the top tier.
